content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if given three side of triangle form a valid traingle.

**Note:** A triangle is valid if sum of its any two sides is greater than the third side. If three sides are a, b and c, then three conditions should be met.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain three space separated integer - the side lengths of a triangle.


Output Format

Output on a single line:

`YES` if the given sides form a valid triangle.
Else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: The only line contains three space‑separated integers a, b and c – the lengths of the sides of a triangle.
content.outputFormat: Print "YES" if the three sides can form a valid triangle, otherwise print "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: All sums are greater than the remaining side → YES.

solution.approach: A triangle is valid iff the sum of any two sides is strictly greater than the third side. We read the three side lengths, check the three inequalities (a+b)>c, (b+c)>a and (c+a)>b, and output YES if all hold, otherwise NO.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int a,b,c;if(scanf("%d %d %d",&a,&b,&c)!=3) return 0; if((a+b)>c && (b+c)>a && (c+a)>b) printf("YES\n"); else printf("NO\n"); return 0; }
